form_description: Grant application for artists, writers, musicians, and creative practitioners seeking funding for a new project

sections.name: artist_info
sections.title: Artist Background
sections.description: Who you are and your creative journey

sections.name: artistic_vision
sections.title: Artistic Vision & Context
sections.description: The deeper meaning and context of your work

sections.name: timeline_budget
sections.title: Timeline & Budget
sections.description: How you'll use the time and money

sections.name: impact
sections.title: Impact & Audience
sections.description: Who will experience your work and why it matters

questions.question_text: Provide a brief biography highlighting your artistic background, training, and significant achievements.
questions.helper_text: 2-3 paragraphs. Include education, exhibitions/publications/performances, awards. Write in third person if required by the grant.

questions.question_text: Describe your artistic practice. What mediums do you work in? What themes recur in your work?
questions.helper_text: This is your chance to help reviewers understand your body of work as a whole. What connects your pieces?

questions.question_text: What drew you to your artistic discipline? Describe a pivotal moment in your creative development.
questions.helper_text: Be genuine. Reviewers read hundreds of applications - authentic stories stand out.

questions.question_text: Describe the creative project you are proposing. What will you create?
questions.helper_text: Be specific and vivid. If it's a series, describe individual pieces. If it's a performance, describe what the audience will experience.

questions.question_text: What is the scope of this project? (e.g., number of pieces, length, scale)
questions.helper_text: Be concrete: '12 paintings, 24x36 inches each' or 'a 90-minute documentary' or 'an album of 10 original compositions'

questions.question_text: What stage is this project currently in? What work have you already completed?
questions.helper_text: Grants often favor projects already in progress. Describe research, sketches, drafts, prototypes.

questions.question_text: What materials, tools, or resources will you need to complete this project?
questions.helper_text: Be specific. This connects to your budget. Include studio space, equipment, materials, collaborators.

questions.question_text: What is the central concept, question, or theme your project explores?
questions.helper_text: Go deeper than description. What ideas drive this work? What are you investigating or expressing?

questions.question_text: How does this project connect to or depart from your previous work?
questions.helper_text: Show evolution. Is this a continuation, a new direction, or a synthesis?

questions.question_text: What artists, movements, or traditions influence this project? How does your work engage with or respond to them?
questions.helper_text: Situate your work in context. Who are your creative ancestors? Who are your contemporaries working on similar questions?

questions.question_text: Why is this the right time for you to create this work?
questions.helper_text: Personal readiness, cultural moment, technical capability - why now?

questions.question_text: Provide a timeline for completing this project. Include major milestones.
questions.helper_text: Be realistic. Include research, creation, revision, and presentation/exhibition phases. Month-by-month or quarter-by-quarter.

questions.question_text: Provide a detailed budget. How will grant funds be used?
questions.helper_text: Be specific: materials ($X), equipment ($X), studio rental ($X), travel ($X), collaborator fees ($X). Show you've thought this through.

questions.question_text: What other funding sources are you pursuing or have secured for this project?
questions.helper_text: Grants like to see leveraged funding. Include in-kind support, other applications pending, personal investment.

questions.question_text: Who is the intended audience for this work? How will they encounter it?
questions.helper_text: Be specific about venue, format, distribution. Gallery? Festival? Online? Publication? Performance space?

questions.question_text: What impact do you hope this work will have? What do you want audiences to feel, think, or do?
questions.helper_text: Be honest about your ambitions. Provoke? Comfort? Challenge? Celebrate? Document?

questions.question_text: How does this project contribute to your community or field?
questions.helper_text: Many grants value community impact. Will you teach? Collaborate? Create access? Preserve traditions? Break new ground?

questions.question_text: Do you have confirmed or potential venues, publishers, or presenters for this work?
questions.helper_text: Letters of interest or commitment strengthen applications. Even 'in conversation with' counts.

questions.question_text: For Work Sample #1: Provide title, date, medium, and a brief description.
questions.helper_text: Describe what the reviewer will see/hear/read. Context matters: was this commissioned? Part of a series? Award-winning?

questions.question_text: For Work Sample #2: Provide title, date, medium, and a brief description.
questions.helper_text: Choose samples that demonstrate range OR depth, depending on what serves your application best.

questions.question_text: For Work Sample #3: Provide title, date, medium, and a brief description.
questions.helper_text: If possible, choose at least one sample that connects thematically or technically to your proposed project.

questions.question_text: How do your work samples demonstrate your readiness to complete the proposed project?
questions.helper_text: Connect the dots for reviewers. Your samples should show you have the skills, vision, and track record to deliver.

business_directions.name: Visual Arts
business_directions.description: Painting, sculpture, photography, installation, mixed media, digital art

business_directions.name: Literary Arts
business_directions.description: Fiction, poetry, creative nonfiction, playwriting, screenwriting

business_directions.name: Performing Arts
business_directions.description: Music composition, choreography, theater, performance art

business_directions.name: Media Arts
business_directions.description: Film, video, audio, interactive media, game design

business_directions.name: Interdisciplinary
business_directions.description: Work that crosses or combines multiple disciplines
